title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Leo reflects on his past with Rachel, who enthusiastically pursued building an artificial family by adopting several children: Mary, Mickey, Tina, Hester, and Jackie. While Leo believed providing a home was worthwhile, he felt the children lacked the ancestral blood, ambition, and integrity of his own family. Jackie, in particular, developed into a manipulative juvenile delinquent. Despite efforts by Rachel and Leo to support him, Jackie’s academic failures and criminal tendencies led to him becoming an angry, threatening man who demanded money from them. Following Rachel's death, Leo found emotional warmth and love in his loyal secretary, Gwendolyn, though he could not marry her while Rachel was alive.

In a separate setting, Donald Craig visits Dr. McMaster to discuss his personal distress regarding his relationship with Hester Aigle. Donald expresses that he loves Hester and believes she loves him, but he is troubled by recent events involving "justice for Jackie Aigles." He suggests that the situation might have been better if certain new evidence had not surfaced. Dr. McMaster notes that many people, including the police chief and a man who recently returned from the South Pole, share this sentiment. Donald reveals that on the night Mr. Calgary visited the Aigle house, Hester called him in a distraught state, canceling their plans to attend a lecture in Dreyfus because she had heard unsettling news.
